papers.title: Low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels
papers.paper_scope: Investigates the bainitic transformation and mechanical performance of five medium-to high-Al steels with varying C contents, alloying elements (Ti, Ni), and austenitizing/austempering parameters.

alloys.alloys_notes: Designed with 0.98 wt% C and 6.00 wt% Al to minimize density and bainitic transformation time. Mo added for hardenability, B for further hardenability improvement.
Slightly higher C content (1.10 wt%) than S1, otherwise similar design. Mo and B also added.
Highest Al content (8.08 wt%), designed to explore very high Al effects on bainitic transformation and mechanical properties. Contains Mo and B.

Ti-micro-alloyed steel with reduced C (0.87 wt%) and Al (5.76 wt%) to accelerate bainitic transformation and improve ductility. Ni (0.78 wt%) added to suppress polygonal ferrite and maintain an αp-free microstructure.
Lower alloying concept with 0.60 wt% C and 3.92 wt% Al, Ni addition (0.78 wt%) to ensure αp-free microstructure without compromising bainitic transformation kinetics.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Heating above 1100 °C at 1 K/s; rapid cooling to suppress intermediate transformations; applied to S1, S2, S4, and S5 (exact duration for S5 austenitizing not specified, assumed similar).

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 300 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Lower austempering temperature than 350 °C; used for S1 and S2.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C for 20 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Higher austenitizing temperature required for S3 to minimize polygonal ferrite.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C for 20 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 270 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Austempering just above Ms (258 °C) for S3; leads to very long transformation time.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumably 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 320 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Used for S4 to explore an intermediate austempering temperature.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1250 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Higher austenitizing temperature for S4 to dissolve TiC/MC precipitates and reduce impingement.

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumed 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 365 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: High austempering temperature for S5, above Ms (331 °C).

processes.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumed 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 335 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
processes.processes_notes: Austempering just above Ms (331 °C) for S5; promotes fine bainitic structure.

processing_steps.method: vacuum arc melting
homogenization at 1100 °C for 24 h with inert argon protection
step-by-step hot rolling to ~11 mm thickness
slow cooling in oven after hot rolling
water jet cutting to cylindrical specimens (φ x 10 mm^3)
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Used OMAX 2626 water jet cutting machine.

processing_steps.method: austenitizing at 1150 °C for 15 min in DIL 805A dilatometer
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Heating rate above 1100 °C: 1 K/s; selected to achieve αp-free microstructure.

processing_steps.method: cooling from 1150 °C to 350 °C at 100 K/s
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Rapid cooling to suppress intermediate phase transformations.

processing_steps.method: isothermal holding at 350 °C for 1600 s (Bt)
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Transformation finish time determined from dilatometric plateau.

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainitic structure with bainitic ferrite laths, retained austenite (γr) as films and blocks, and (Fe,M)xCy precipitates near phase boundaries.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, nano-bainitic, interlocked Type 1 and Type 2 lamellae
film-like at lath boundaries and blocky regions between bainite packets
rounded, located near γr/αb boundaries; no micro-void formation observed

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainitic microstructure with finer laths, retained austenite, and similar precipitates.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, average true thickness Lt(t) 35 nm
film-like, lower volume fraction compared to 350 °C treatment
rounded, similar to 350 °C condition

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainitic structure with slightly coarser laths (Lt(t) 68 nm), high retained austenite (34%), and (Fe,M)xCy precipitates.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, Type 1 and Type 2, average Lt(t) 68 nm
film-like and blocky
rounded, at interfaces

structures.overall_structure: Fine nano-bainite with Lt(t) 34 nm, Vγ 29%, microstrain εγ 0.47%, εα 0.44%.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, true thickness 34 nm
film-like
rounded

structures.overall_structure: Dual-phase structure with polygonal ferrite (αp ~8.2%), nano-bainite, and retained austenite. Sluggish γr observed.

phases_present.morphology: allotriomorphic and occasional idiomorphic plates; α or δ nature unclear, referred to as αp
lath-like, Lt(t) 58 nm
mixed film-like and blocky; sluggish transformation regions with C-enriched γr near αp
rounded, detected near boundaries

structures.overall_structure: Fine bainitic ferrite (Lt(t) 40 nm) with retained austenite (29%) and pronounced strain partitioning (εγ 0.54%, εα 0.33%). αp present. Ductility severely reduced.

phases_present.morphology: allotriomorphic, similar to 350 °C condition but volume fraction not explicitly given; αp persists
lath-like, Lt(t) 40 nm; higher micro-strain in γr (0.54%) than in αb (0.33%)
mixed, with partially decomposed sluggish γr near αp boundaries
rounded, EDX confirmed (Fig. 9(g)(j))

defects.type: dislocation accumulation in γr leading to strain partitioning

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainite with TiMoC precipitates aligned in rolling direction, minimal αp (<1%), and reduced (Fe,M)xCy.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, Lt(t) 56 nm; impingement of bainite sheaves observed
film-like and blocky
longitudinally aligned, indicating formation during cooling after casting and subsequent hot rolling deformation

precipitates.distribution: elongated clusters along prior deformation direction; no micro-defects observed around them

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainite with TiMoC precipitates, Lt(t) 36 nm, Vγ 26%.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, true thickness 36 nm
film-like and blocky
longitudinal alignment

structures.overall_structure: Nano-bainite with TiMoC and reduced impingement due to higher austenitizing temperature; Lt(t) 58 nm, Vγ 29%.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like, thickness 58 nm
film-like and blocky
elongated, partly dissolved at 1250 °C but re-precipitated during cooling

structures.overall_structure: Bainitic structure with slightly coarser laths (Lt(t) 55 nm), film-like γr, and trace allotriomorphic ferrite (F). No martensite. Rapid transformation, minimal impingement.

phases_present.morphology: lath-like packets, slightly coarser than at 335 °C; KAM map shows marginally higher global KAM
predominantly film-like at lath boundaries; no blocky γr reported
allotriomorphic ferrite (labelled F in EBSD), very minor

structures.overall_structure: Fine nano-bainite with Lt(t) 41 nm, lenticular martensite islands scattered, lower γr (19%), and higher microstrain in γr (0.51%). Local KAM spikes near M.

phases_present.morphology: narrow lath packets; KAM hot-spots at boundaries and near martensite
film-like; volume fraction undercounted by EBSD, XRD value 19%
sporadic lenticular plates; form stress-assisted transformation slightly above Ms (331 °C)

defects.type: geometrically necessary dislocations near M and lath boundaries

interfaces.phase_evolution: γr films at bainitic lath boundaries provide TRIP effect; (Fe,M)xCy precipitates at interfaces do not induce micro-voids and have negligible negative effect.

interface_notes.slippage_situation: C-partitioning from αp into adjacent γr locally stabilizes austenite and inhibits bainite growth, leading to sluggish transformation and incomplete reaction in some regions.

phases.coherence: favorable wettability, no micro-defects at interface

interface_notes.stress_strain_distribution: Impingement of bainite sheaves due to increased phase boundaries from TiMoC precipitates; this limits acceleration of transformation after nucleation.

defect_interaction.interaction_type: high GND density at M interfaces
defect_interaction.quality_description: Local KAM hot-spots adjacent to martensite indicate stress concentration and geometrically necessary dislocations.

yield_strength.value: not explicitly given; generally correlated with UTS

characterization_methods.technique: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure characterization, phase quantification, precipitate analysis, transformation kinetics
characterization_methods.key_findings: Nano-bainitic structure with Lt(t) 55 nm, Vγ 34%, εγ 0.35%, εα 0.45%. (Fe,M)xCy precipitates observed at γr/αb boundaries without micro-voids.
Finer bainite (35 nm), Vγ 27%, higher hardness.
Coarser laths (68 nm), Vγ 34%, similar precipitates.

characterization_methods.technique: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
characterization_methods.key_findings: αp ~8.2%, bainite 58 nm, Vγ 34%, sluggish transformation regions.
Bainite 40 nm, Vγ 29%, strong strain partitioning (εγ 0.54%, εα 0.33%), ductility drop.

characterization_methods.technique: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
characterization_methods.key_findings: TiMoC precipitates longitudinally aligned, bainite 56 nm, Vγ 25%, impingement observed.
Bainite 55 nm, Vγ 26%, film-like γr, trace allotriomorphic ferrite, no martensite.
Bainite 41 nm, Vγ 19%, lenticular martensite locally, γr microstrain 0.51%, KAM hot-spots near M.

computational_details.simulation_method: Precipitation kinetics simulation (TTP diagrams) and equilibrium phase calculations using MatCalc software (v6.01). Based on heterogeneous nucleation theory, ortho-equilibrium transformation, considering dislocation and grain boundary sites. Volumetric misfit considered. Phases: fcc, bcc, liquid, κ-carbide, hcp carbide, AlN, TiCN–TiMoC system.
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Microstructural precipitate analysis confirmed primary formation of (Fe,M)xCy and TiMoC; simulations supported findings, indicating primary precipitation above austempering temperatures at austenitizing and cooling stages.

unmapped_findings: Density reduction by Al: approximately 1.3% per 1 wt.% Al addition (from literature); not directly measured for these alloys.
Table 2 compiles MatCalc-predicted, measured, and applied TA temperatures and Ms values for each steel.
For S3 at TB 270 °C, bainitic transformation required 43,200 s; an intermediate microstructure at 25,200 s still showed incomplete transformation.
LOM images at 1050 °C benchmark show αp retention in S1, S3, S4, S5; S2 is αp-free at this temperature.

Figure captions indicate extensive microscopy (Fig. 9,10,11) and simulation results (Fig. 4,8) that could not be fully captured due to lack of image pixel data; trends and labeled features are included where explicitly stated in captions or text.
